title: Understanding decomposing figures to find volume
description: Learn how to decompose complex 3D figures into simpler rectangular prisms to find their volume. Then learn to break down the figures, calculate the volume of each prism, and add them together to find the total volume.
topic_path: Module 5: Addition and multiplication with volume and area
Topic B: Volume and the operations of multiplication and addition
Understanding decomposing figures to find volume

assessment_items.question_content:
The figure below is made up of two rectangular prisms. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which expression can be used to find the volume of the figure?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: To find the volume of a rectangular prism, we multiply the $\text{length}\times\text{height}\times{\text{width}}$.

Let's break the figure into $2$ parts.

[[☃ image 1]]

The first prism has dimensions of $\blueE2\text{ cm}, \blueE9\text{ cm}$ and $\blueE5\text{ cm}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So, to find the volume we use the expression $\blueE2\text{ cm}\times\blueE9\text{ cm}\times\blueE5\text{ cm}$.

The second prism has dimensions of $\purpleD8\text{ cm}, \purpleD9\text{ cm}$ and $\purpleD1\text{ cm}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

So, to find the volume we use the expression $\purpleD8\text{ cm}\times\purpleD9\text{ cm}\times\purpleD1\text{ cm}$.

To find the total volume, we add both volumes together.

$(\blueE2\text{ cm}\times\blueE9\text{ cm}\times\blueE5\text{ cm}) + (\purpleD1\text{ cm}\times\purpleD8\text{ cm}\times\purpleD9\text{ cm})$

options.alt: A figure made up of 2 rectangular prisms. Color is used to show the rectangular prisms that make up the figure. The first rectangular prism is blue and has the dimensions 9 centimeters length by 5 centimeters width by 2 centimeters height. The second rectangular prism is purple and has the dimensions 9 centimeters length by 1 centimeter width by 8 centimeters height.

choices.content: $(\blueE2\text{ cm}\times\blueE9\text{ cm}\times\blueE5\text{ cm}) + (\purpleD1\text{ cm}\times\purpleD8\text{ cm}\times\purpleD9\text{ cm})$
$(\blueE2\text{ cm}\times\blueE9\text{ cm}\times\blueE5\text{ cm}) + (\purpleD2\text{ cm}\times\purpleD8\text{ cm}\times\purpleD5\text{ cm})$
$(\blueE2\text{ cm}\times\blueE9\text{ cm}\times\blueE5\text{ cm}) + (\purpleD1\text{ cm}\times\purpleD8\text{ cm}\times\purpleD5\text{ cm})$
$(\blueE2\text{ cm}\times\blueE9\text{ cm}\times\blueE5\text{ cm}) + (\purpleD2\text{ cm}\times\purpleD8\text{ cm}\times\purpleD9\text{ cm})$

assessment_items.question_content:
The figure below is made up of two rectangular prisms. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which expression can be used to find the volume of the figure?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: To find the volume of a rectangular prism, we multiply the $\text{length}\times\text{height}\times{\text{width}}$.

Let's break the figure into $2$ parts.

[[☃ image 1]]

The first prism has dimensions of $\blueE5\text{ cm}, \blueE{12}\text{ cm}$ and $\blueE5\text{ cm}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So, to find the volume we use the expression $\blueE5\text{ cm}\times\blueE{12}\text{ cm}\times\blueE5\text{ cm}$.

The second prism has dimensions of $\purpleD{12}\text{ cm}, \purpleD{10}\text{ cm}$ and $\purpleD3\text{ cm}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So, to find the volume we use the expression $\purpleD{12}\text{ cm}\times\purpleD{10}\text{ cm}\times\purpleD3\text{ cm}$.

To find the total volume, we add both volumes together.

$(\blueE5\text{ cm}\times\blueE{12}\text{ cm}\times\blueE5\text{ cm}) + (\purpleD3\text{ cm}\times\purpleD{10}\text{ cm}\times\purpleD{12}\text{ cm})$

options.alt: A figure made up of 2 rectangular prisms. Color is used to show the rectangular prisms that make up the figure. The first rectangular prism is blue and has the dimensions 12 centimeters length by 5 centimeters width by 5 centimeters height. The second rectangular prism is purple and has the dimensions 12 centimeters length by 3 centimeters width by 10 centimeters height.

choices.content: $(\blueE5\text{ cm}\times\blueE{12}\text{ cm}\times\blueE5\text{ cm}) + (\purpleD3\text{ cm}\times\purpleD{10}\text{ cm}\times\purpleD{12}\text{ cm})$
$(\blueE5\text{ cm}\times\blueE5\text{ cm}\times\blueE{12}\text{ cm}) + (\purpleD3\text{ cm}\times\purpleD{10}\text{ cm} \times\purpleD{10}\text{ cm}) $
$(\blueE{12}\text{ cm}\times\blueE5\text{ cm}\times\blueE5\text{ cm}) + (\purpleD5\text{ cm}\times\purpleD3\text{ cm}\times\purpleD{10}\text{ cm})$

assessment_items.question_content:
The figure below is made up of two rectangular prisms. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which expression can be used to find the volume of the figure?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: To find the volume of a rectangular prism, we multiply the $\text{length}\times\text{height}\times{\text{width}}$.

Let's break the figure into $2$ parts.

[[☃ image 1]]

The first prism has dimensions of $\blueE2\text{ cm}, \blueE{8}\text{ cm}$ and $\blueE7\text{ cm}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So, to find the volume we use the expression $\blueE2\text{ cm}\times\blueE{8}\text{ cm}\times\blueE7\text{ cm}$.

The second prism has dimensions of $\purpleD{8}\text{ cm}, \purpleD{6}\text{ cm}$ and $\purpleD1\text{ cm}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So, to find the volume we use the expression $\purpleD{1}\text{ cm}\times\purpleD{6}\text{ cm}\times\purpleD8\text{ cm}$.

To find the total volume, we add both volumes together.

$(\blueE2\text{ cm}\times\blueE{8}\text{ cm}\times\blueE7\text{ cm}) + (\purpleD1\text{ cm}\times\purpleD{6}\text{ cm}\times\purpleD{8}\text{ cm})$

options.alt: A figure made up of 2 rectangular prisms. Color is used to show the rectangular prisms that make up the figure. The first rectangular prism is blue and has the dimensions 8 centimeters length by 7 centimeters width by 2 centimeters height. The second rectangular prism is purple and has the dimensions 8 centimeters length by 1 centimeter width by 6 centimeters height.

choices.content: $(\blueE2\text{ cm}\times\blueE{8}\text{ cm}\times\blueE7\text{ cm}) + (\purpleD1\text{ cm}\times\purpleD{6}\text{ cm}\times\purpleD{8}\text{ cm})$
$(\blueE2\text{ cm}\times\blueE7\text{ cm} \times\blueE6\text{ cm}) + (\purpleD1\text{ cm}\times\purpleD{6}\text{ cm}\times\purpleD{8}\text{ cm})$
$(\blueE2\text{ cm}\times\blueE{7}\text{ cm}\times\blueE1\text{ cm}) + (\purpleD1\text{ cm}\times\purpleD{6}\text{ cm}\times\purpleD{8}\text{ cm})$

assessment_items.question_content:
The figure below is made up of two rectangular prisms. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which expression can be used to find the volume of the figure?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: To find the volume of a rectangular prism, we multiply the $\text{length}\times\text{height}\times{\text{width}}$.

Let's break the figure into $2$ parts.

[[☃ image 1]]

The first prism has dimensions of $\goldE1\text{ cm}, \goldE{5}\text{ cm}$ and $\goldE3\text{ cm}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So, to find the volume we use the expression $\goldE1\text{ cm}\times\goldE{5}\text{ cm}\times\goldE3\text{ cm}$.

The second prism has dimensions of $\purpleD{5}\text{ cm}, \purpleD{7}\text{ cm}$ and $\purpleD4\text{ cm}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So, to find the volume we use the expression $\purpleD{4}\text{ cm}\times\purpleD{7}\text{ cm}\times\purpleD5\text{ cm}$.

To find the total volume, we add both volumes together.

$(\goldE1\text{ cm}\times\goldE{5}\text{ cm}\times\goldE3\text{ cm}) + (\purpleD4\text{ cm}\times\purpleD{7}\text{ cm}\times\purpleD{5}\text{ cm})$

options.alt: A figure made up of 2 rectangular prisms. Color is used to show the rectangular prisms that make up the figure. The first rectangular prism is gold and has the dimensions 5 centimeters length by 3 centimeters width by 1 centimeter height. The second rectangular prism is purple and has the dimensions 5 centimeters length by 4 centimeter width by 7 centimeters height.

choices.content: $(\goldE1\text{ cm}\times\goldE{5}\text{ cm}\times\goldE3\text{ cm}) + (\purpleD4\text{ cm}\times\purpleD{7}\text{ cm}\times\purpleD{5}\text{ cm})$
$(\goldE1\text{ cm}\times\goldE{4}\text{ cm}\times\goldE3\text{ cm}) + (\purpleD4\text{ cm}\times\purpleD{7}\text{ cm}\times\purpleD{5}\text{ cm})$
$(\goldE1\text{ cm}\times\goldE{7}\text{ cm}\times\goldE3\text{ cm})+ (\purpleD4\text{ cm}\times\purpleD{7}\text{ cm}\times\purpleD{5}\text{ cm})$

The first prism has dimensions of $\blueE3\text{ cm}, \blueE{7}\text{ cm}$ and $\blueE5\text{ cm}$.

So, to find the volume we use the expression $\blueE3\text{ cm}\times\blueE{7}\text{ cm}\times\blueE5\text{ cm}$.

The second prism has dimensions of $\goldE{3}\text{ cm}, \goldE{8}\text{ cm}$ and $\goldE7\text{ cm}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So, to find the volume we use the expression $\goldE{3}\text{ cm}\times\goldE{7}\text{ cm}\times\goldE8\text{ cm}$.

To find the total volume, we add both volumes together.

options.alt: A figure made up of 2 rectangular prisms. Color is used to show the rectangular prisms that make up the figure. The first rectangular prism is blue and has the dimensions 5 centimeters length by 7 centimeters width by 3 centimeters height. The second rectangular prism is gold and has the dimensions 8 centimeters height by 3 centimeter width by 7 inches length.

choices.content: $(\blueE3\text{ cm}\times\blueE{7}\text{ cm}\times\blueE5\text{ cm}) + (\goldE3\text{ cm}\times\goldE{7}\text{ cm}\times\goldE{8}\text{ cm})$
$(\blueE3\text{ cm}\times\blueE{7}\text{ cm}\times\blueE5\text{ cm}) + (\goldE3\text{ cm}\times\goldE{3}\text{ cm}\times\goldE{8}\text{ cm})$
$(\blueE3\text{ cm}\times\blueE{7}\text{ cm}\times\blueE5\text{ cm}) +(\goldE3\text{ cm}\times\goldE{5}\text{ cm}\times\goldE{8}\text{ cm})$

assessment_items.question_content:
The figure below is made up of two rectangular prisms. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which expression can be used to find the volume of the figure?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: To find the volume of a rectangular prism, we multiply the $\text{length}\times\text{height}\times{\text{width}}$.

Let's break the figure into $2$ parts.

[[☃ image 1]]

The first prism has dimensions of $\blueE4\text{ m}, \blueE6\text{ m}$ and $\blueE2\text{ m}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So, to find the volume we use the expression $\blueE4\text{ m}\times\blueE6\text{ m}\times\blueE2\text{ m}$.

The second prism has dimensions of $\maroonE6\text{ m}, \maroonE7\text{ m}$ and $\maroonE3\text{ m}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

So, to find the volume we use the expression $\maroonE6\text{ m}\times\maroonE7\text{ m}\times\maroonE3\text{ m}$.

To find the total volume, we add both volumes together.

$(\blueE4\text{ m}\times\blueE6\text{ m}\times\blueE2\text{ m}) + (\maroonE6\text{ m}\times\maroonE7\text{ m}\times\maroonE3\text{ m})$

options.alt: A figure made up of 2 rectangular prisms. Color is used to show the rectangular prisms that make up the figure. The first rectangular prism is blue and has the dimensions 6 meters length by 2 meters width by 4 meters height. The second rectangular prism is maroon and has the dimensions 6 meters length by 3 meters width by 7 meters height.

choices.content: $(\blueE4\text{ m}\times\blueE6\text{ m}\times\blueE2\text{ m}) + (\maroonE6\text{ m}\times\maroonE7\text{ m}\times\maroonE3\text{ m})$
$(\blueE4\text{ m}\times\blueE7\text{ m}\times\blueE2\text{ m}) + (\maroonE6\text{ m}\times\maroonE7\text{ m}\times\maroonE3\text{ m})$
$(\blueE4\text{ m}\times\blueE3\text{ m}\times\blueE2\text{ m}) + (\maroonE6\text{ m}\times\maroonE7\text{ m}\times\maroonE3\text{ m})$

assessment_items.question_content:
The figure below is made up of two rectangular prisms. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which expression can be used to find the volume of the figure?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: To find the volume of a rectangular prism, we multiply the $\text{length}\times\text{height}\times{\text{width}}$.

Let's break the figure into $2$ parts.


[[☃ image 1]]

The first prism has the dimensions of $\purpleD3\text{ m}, \purpleD6\text{ m}$ and $\purpleD2\text{ m}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

So, to find the volume we multiply $\purpleD3\text{ m}\times\purpleD6\text{ m}\times\purpleD2\text{ m}$.

The volume for this prism is $\purpleD{36}\text{ cubic meters}$.

The second prism has the dimensions of $\goldE6\text{ m}, \goldE{10}\text{ m}$ and $\goldE6\text{ m}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

So, to find the volume we use the expression $\goldE6\text{ m}\times\goldE{10}\text{ m}\times\goldE6\text{ m}$.

The volume for this prism is $\goldE{360}\text{ cubic meters}$.

To find the total volume, we add both volumes together.

$\purpleD{36} \text{ cubic meters}+\goldE{360}\text{ cubic meters}$

options.alt: A figure made up of 2 rectangular prisms. Color is used to show the rectangular prisms that make up the figure. The first rectangular prism is purple and has the dimensions 2 meters length by 6 meters width by 3 meters height. The second rectangular prism is orange and has the dimensions 6 meters length by 6 meters width by 10 meters height.

So, to find the volume we use the expression $\blueE3\text{ m}\times\blueE{4}\text{ m}\times\blueE5\text{ m}$.

The volume for this prism is $\blueE{60}\text{ cubic meters}$.

The second prism has the dimensions of $\maroonE5\text{ m}, \maroonE{10}\text{ m}$ and $\maroonE2\text{ m}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So, to find the volume we multiply $\maroonE5\text{ m}\times\maroonE{10}\text{ m}\times\maroonE{2}\text{ m}$.

To find the total volume, we add both volumes together.

$\blueE{60} \text{ cubic meters}+\maroonE{100}\text{ cubic meters}$
